name: Coal and Petroleum
subject: chemistry
summary: Fossil fuels formed over millions of years from dead organisms. Coal, petroleum, natural gas — and why we must conserve them.

crushed.title: Fossil fuels = ancient energy
crushed.text: Coal, petroleum, natural gas formed from dead plants/animals buried under heat & pressure for millions of years.

crushed.title: Coal types
crushed.text: Peat → Lignite → Bituminous → Anthracite. More carbon = more energy as you go up.

crushed.title: Petroleum = crude oil
crushed.text: Dark viscous liquid. Refineries separate it into petrol, diesel, kerosene, LPG by fractional distillation.

crushed.title: Burning hydrocarbons
crushed.text: CH₄ + 2O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O + energy. Fossil fuels are mainly carbon & hydrogen compounds.

crushed.title: Non-renewable — conserve!
crushed.text: Takes millions of years to form, seconds to burn. Use judiciously, explore solar/wind alternatives.

items.label: Coal
items.detail: Solid. Formed from dead plant matter in swamps. Used in power plants & steel industry

items.label: Petroleum
items.detail: Liquid. Formed from marine microorganisms. Refined into fuels & plastics

items.label: Natural gas
items.detail: Gaseous. Mainly methane (CH₄). Stored under high pressure as CNG

items.label: Peat
items.detail: First stage — least carbon, most moisture

items.label: Bituminous
items.detail: Most commonly used — good heat value

items.label: Anthracite
items.detail: Highest carbon content — best quality coal

demos.title: Combustion of Methane (Natural Gas)
demos.description: Methane burns completely in oxygen — main reaction when you use LPG/CNG stove.

steps.label: Gas released
steps.description: Methane flows from LPG/CNG supply to burner

steps.label: Spark/ignition
steps.description: Lighter or spark ignites the gas-air mixture

steps.label: Blue flame
steps.description: Complete combustion — clean blue flame on stove

steps.label: Products
steps.description: CO₂ and water vapour released — energy heats your food

demos.title: Burning a Hydrocarbon (Petrol)
demos.description: Petrol (a mix of hydrocarbons) burns in car engines to release energy.

steps.label: Fuel injected
steps.description: Petrol vapour mixed with air in engine cylinder

steps.label: Spark ignites
steps.description: Spark plug creates ignition at exact moment

steps.label: Explosion
steps.description: Rapid combustion pushes piston — engine power!

steps.label: Exhaust
steps.description: CO₂ and H₂O exit through exhaust pipe

flows.title: How Coal Formed Over Millions of Years
flows.description: From ancient swamp plants to the coal we mine today.

steps.label: 300 mya
steps.description: Huge forests of ferns & trees grow in warm, wet swamps.

steps.label: Burial
steps.description: Dead plants pile up, buried under mud and sand.

steps.label: Pressure + heat
steps.description: Layers compress the plant matter; heat drives out moisture & gases.

steps.label: Coal forms
steps.description: Plant material transforms into peat → lignite → bituminous → anthracite.

steps.label: Mining
steps.description: Coal mined from underground — burned for electricity & industry.

flows.title: Fractional Distillation of Petroleum
flows.description: Crude oil heated in a fractionating column separates into useful fractions by boiling point.

steps.label: Crude oil
steps.description: Thick crude oil vapourised in a furnace at the base of the column.

steps.label: Column
steps.description: Column gets cooler towards the top. Different hydrocarbons condense at different heights.

steps.label: Top fractions
steps.description: Small molecules (low boiling point) condense near the top — petrol, LPG.

steps.label: Middle
steps.description: Medium-sized molecules condense in the middle sections.

steps.label: Bottom
steps.description: Large molecules (high boiling point) collected at the bottom.

keyTerms.definition: Fuel formed from remains of dead organisms over geological time
keyTerms.analogy: Ancient sunlight stored in chemical bonds

keyTerms.definition: Separating mixture components by different boiling points
keyTerms.analogy: Sorting passengers by height at different floor levels

keyTerms.definition: Compound of hydrogen and carbon only
keyTerms.analogy: The building blocks of all fossil fuels

keyTerms.definition: Compressed Natural Gas — mainly methane, stored at high pressure
keyTerms.analogy: Natural gas squeezed into a cylinder for vehicles

chapterSummary.overview: Coal and petroleum are fossil fuels formed over millions of years from dead organisms. This chapter explains their formation, types of coal, refining of petroleum into useful fractions, natural gas, and why we must use these resources judiciously.
keyPoints: Coal forms from buried plant matter under heat and pressure over 300 million years.
Types: peat → lignite → bituminous → anthracite (increasing carbon content).
Petroleum is refined by fractional distillation into LPG, petrol, kerosene, diesel, lubricants.
Coal tar gives dyes, drugs, explosives; coke is used in steel manufacture.
Fossil fuels are non-renewable; burning them causes air pollution and global warming.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Describe formation of coal and petroleum. List petroleum fractions and uses. Explain why fossil fuels must be conserved.
